question.text: Under SEBI (SAST) Regulations, 2011, what is the creeping acquisition limit for an acquirer already holding 25% or more voting rights?

answer.text: Additional 5% voting rights within any financial year
Additional 10% voting rights within any financial year
Additional 2% voting rights within any financial year
No additional acquisition is permitted without an open offer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 3(2), an acquirer holding 25% or more but less than maximum permissible non-public shareholding cannot acquire additional shares entitling more than 5% voting rights within any financial year without making a public announcement of an open offer. **The Trap:** The 5% is not a blanket exemption — exceeding it triggers mandatory open offer obligations. It applies only within a single financial year. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compliance officers must track incremental acquisitions by promoters/acquirers on a financial year basis to ensure timely compliance with open offer obligations.
